text:
There once was a boy named Billy. Every weekend, Billy would venture into the woods just down the street. There he loved discovering old trees with beautiful bark.

One summer afternoon, Billy found an oak tree hollow. It smelled musty! Perhaps an old squirrel lived there? Billy climbed up.

Inside lived an oddball squirrel! She was blind from a eye disease, but her tail was impeccable.

"What brings you into my owl house?" asked OWlmouse.

"Oh just exploring," said Billy.

"Why do you always sit here?" she asked.

"To watch the birds."

"Stars!"

"Why?"

"Oh. You like them?"

"Oh yes."

They chatted about everything.

That summer, as Fall turned chilly, OWlmouse decided she couldn't stick around forever. She gathered seeds.

"I have something special."

"What is it?"

OWL feathers.

"You keep these hidden? You could sell them."

"I could."

"Don't."

"Why not?"

"You'll just want them later."

Billy didn't listen
